title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Practice Set 17: Logic and Puzzles
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: How many stars are shown in the picture below?
questions.image_description: A row of five identical stars.
questions.solution: Count each star one by one: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. There are 5 stars.

questions.question_text: Lea has 3 red balloons and 4 blue balloons. How many balloons does she have in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 70' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='20' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='50' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='80' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='120' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='150' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='180' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='210' cy='25' r='12'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three balloons on the left and four balloons on the right.
questions.solution: Add the two groups: 3 + 4 = 7 balloons.

questions.question_text: Which figure below is a triangle?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='5' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><text x='15' y='58' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>A</text><circle cx='70' cy='30' r='15'/><text x='65' y='58' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>B</text><polygon points='115,45 130,15 145,45'/><text x='125' y='58' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>C</text><polygon points='170,15 200,15 200,45 170,45'/><text x='180' y='58' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>D</text><ellipse cx='235' cy='30' rx='18' ry='12'/><text x='230' y='58' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>E</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Five shapes labeled A through E: a square, a circle, a triangle, a rectangle, and an oval.
questions.solution: A triangle has three sides. Shape C is the only one with three sides.

questions.question_text: What number comes next in the pattern: 2, 4, 6, 8, ___ ?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='10' y='25' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>2, 4, 6, 8, ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: The number sequence 2, 4, 6, 8 with a question mark.
questions.solution: The pattern adds 2 each time. After 8 comes 8 + 2 = 10.

questions.question_text: Which number is the greatest?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='10' y='25' font-size='16' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>7   12   9   15   11</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Five numbers displayed: 7, 12, 9, 15, 11.
questions.solution: Compare all five numbers. 15 is larger than 7, 12, 9, and 11.

questions.question_text: A clock shows 3 o'clock. How many hours until 7 o'clock?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 80 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='35'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='40' y2='15'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='60' y2='40'/><text x='36' y='12' font-size='8' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>12</text><text x='68' y='44' font-size='8' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>3</text><text x='37' y='76' font-size='8' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>6</text><text x='6' y='44' font-size='8' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>9</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A clock face showing the time 3 o'clock.
questions.solution: From 3 to 7, count forward: 4, 5, 6, 7. That is 4 hours.

questions.question_text: How many circles are in the picture?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='50' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='80' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='110' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='140' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='170' cy='30' r='10'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A row of six circles.
questions.solution: Count each circle: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. There are 6 circles.

questions.question_text: What is 10 minus 3?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='60' y='30' font-size='20' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>10 - 3 = ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: The arithmetic expression 10 minus 3.
questions.solution: Subtract: 10 - 3 = 7.

questions.question_text: Anna, Ben, and Chloe stand in a line. Anna is not first. Ben is last. Who is first?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='20' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='85' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='150' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><text x='28' y='35' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>1st</text><text x='93' y='35' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>2nd</text><text x='158' y='35' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>3rd</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three positions labeled 1st, 2nd, and 3rd.
questions.solution: Ben is last (3rd). Anna is not first, so Anna is 2nd. Chloe must be first.

questions.question_text: How many squares are in the grid shown (include the large one)?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 80 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='10' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='30' height='30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 2 by 2 grid made of four small squares forming one large square.
questions.solution: There are 4 small squares plus 1 large square made by the outer border. Total 5 squares.

questions.question_text: Tom has 8 apples. He gives 2 to his sister and 3 to his brother. How many apples does Tom have left?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='20' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='45' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='70' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='95' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='120' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='145' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='170' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='195' cy='25' r='10'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A row of eight apples.
questions.solution: Tom gives away 2 + 3 = 5 apples. He had 8, so 8 - 5 = 3 apples left.

questions.question_text: Look at the pattern: circle, square, circle, square, circle, ___ . What shape comes next?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='10'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><circle cx='80' cy='20' r='10'/><rect x='100' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><circle cx='140' cy='20' r='10'/><text x='165' y='25' font-size='16' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Alternating circles and squares followed by a question mark.
questions.solution: The pattern alternates circle, square. After circle comes square.

questions.question_text: Maya is taller than Nora. Nora is taller than Omar. Who is the shortest?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 90' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='10' y='20' width='30' height='60'/><rect x='60' y='35' width='30' height='45'/><rect x='110' y='50' width='30' height='30'/><text x='15' y='15' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>Maya</text><text x='65' y='30' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>Nora</text><text x='113' y='45' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>Omar</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three bars showing Maya tallest, Nora medium, Omar shortest.
questions.solution: Maya is taller than Nora, and Nora is taller than Omar, so Omar is the shortest.

questions.question_text: How many black dots are on the domino piece?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='5' y='5' width='110' height='50'/><line x1='60' y1='5' x2='60' y2='55'/><circle cx='25' cy='20' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='80' cy='15' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='95' cy='15' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='80' cy='45' r='4' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='95' cy='45' r='4' fill='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A domino with 2 dots on the left side and 4 dots on the right side.
questions.solution: Left has 2 dots, right has 4 dots. Total: 2 + 4 = 6 dots.

questions.question_text: A class starts at 9 o'clock and lasts 2 hours. When does the class end?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 80 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='35'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='40' y2='15'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='25' y2='25'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A clock showing 9 o'clock with hour hand near 9.
questions.solution: Starting at 9 and adding 2 hours: 9 + 2 = 11 o'clock.

questions.question_text: In a box there are red and blue marbles. There are 4 red marbles. The total is 9. How many are blue?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 140 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='10' y='20' width='120' height='50'/><text x='40' y='50' font-size='14' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>? marbles</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A box holding marbles of two colors.
questions.solution: Total 9 minus red 4 equals 5 blue marbles.

questions.question_text: Three friends each have a different pet: a cat, a dog, and a fish. Sam does not have a dog. Eli has the fish. Who has the dog?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='20' y='30' font-size='12' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>Sam</text><text x='90' y='30' font-size='12' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>Eli</text><text x='160' y='30' font-size='12' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>Rob</text><line x1='15' y1='40' x2='55' y2='40'/><line x1='85' y1='40' x2='115' y2='40'/><line x1='155' y1='40' x2='195' y2='40'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Names Sam, Eli, and Rob on a line of three pet owners.
questions.solution: Eli has the fish. Sam does not have the dog, so Sam has the cat. Rob must have the dog.

questions.question_text: How many triangles can you count in this figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><polygon points='50,10 10,70 90,70'/><line x1='50' y1='10' x2='50' y2='70'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle with a vertical line from the top vertex to the middle of the bottom, dividing it into two smaller triangles.
questions.solution: There are 2 small triangles plus the outer large triangle, making 3 triangles.

questions.question_text: A number plus 5 equals 12. What is the number?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='40' y='30' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>? + 5 = 12</text></svg>
questions.image_description: An equation with a missing number plus 5 equals 12.

questions.question_text: In the pattern 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, what is the 6th number?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='10' y='25' font-size='16' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>1, 3, 5, 7, 9, ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A sequence of odd numbers with the 6th number missing.
questions.solution: Each number increases by 2. After 9 comes 9 + 2 = 11.

questions.question_text: A frog jumps 2 lily pads forward, then 1 back. After 3 such jump cycles, how many pads has it moved forward in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='20' cy='25' r='8'/><circle cx='50' cy='25' r='8'/><circle cx='80' cy='25' r='8'/><circle cx='110' cy='25' r='8'/><circle cx='140' cy='25' r='8'/><circle cx='170' cy='25' r='8'/><circle cx='200' cy='25' r='8'/><circle cx='230' cy='25' r='8'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A row of eight lily pads for the frog to jump across.
questions.solution: Each cycle nets +2 - 1 = +1 pad. After 3 cycles: 3 × 1 = 3 pads forward.

questions.question_text: Four children have ages 6, 8, 5, and 9. What is the difference between the oldest and the youngest?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='20' y='30' font-size='16' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>6   8   5   9</text></svg>
questions.image_description: The ages 6, 8, 5, and 9 shown in a row.
questions.solution: Oldest is 9, youngest is 5. Difference: 9 - 5 = 4.

questions.question_text: A box contains 3 red, 2 green, and 4 yellow crayons. How many crayons are in the box?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='10' y='20' width='140' height='50'/><rect x='20' y='30' width='8' height='30'/><rect x='32' y='30' width='8' height='30'/><rect x='44' y='30' width='8' height='30'/><rect x='58' y='30' width='8' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='30' width='8' height='30'/><rect x='84' y='30' width='8' height='30'/><rect x='96' y='30' width='8' height='30'/><rect x='108' y='30' width='8' height='30'/><rect x='120' y='30' width='8' height='30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A box of crayons standing upright.
questions.solution: Add all groups: 3 + 2 + 4 = 9 crayons.

questions.question_text: Four kangaroos sit in a row. The tallest sits at one end. The shortest sits next to the tallest. The second tallest is at the other end. Where is the second shortest?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='10' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='110' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='160' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><text x='22' y='35' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>1</text><text x='72' y='35' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>2</text><text x='122' y='35' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>3</text><text x='172' y='35' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>4</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Four positions labeled 1, 2, 3, and 4 in a row.
questions.solution: Tallest is at position 1 (an end). Shortest is next to tallest at position 2. Second tallest is at the other end, position 4. So second shortest is at position 3.
